summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects and blocks project file mutations from full-tool Claude agents

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA-256 digests of all project files; diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after to detect changes
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation detects any file mutations during agent execution

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshots taken before/after agent execution; changes detected and reported in result.boundary.changes
evidence.why: Runtime boundary enforcement triggers when agents mutate project files

evidence.summary: Boundary violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that prevent PRD readiness
evidence.why: Boundary violations block PRD readiness even when agent results pass

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines writes_business_code=false and agent_instruction='boundary_controlled'
evidence.why: Dispatch plan specifies forbidden project mutations at protocol level

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() includes 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' in agent instructions
evidence.why: Agent prompts explicitly forbid project mutations in hard rules

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues() generates blockers from missing/conflicting evidence agent results
evidence.why: PRD readiness inspection enforces evidence quality and completeness

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() blocks external-only evidence for existing-project facts
evidence.why: Ensures brownfield claims require project-scoped evidence from code/tests/docs

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Test evidence that boundary audit overrides passing agent results on violations

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test confirms untracked project mutations are detected and blocked
evidence.why: Boundary audit works with both tracked and untracked files

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt() handles claude/codex/custom provider execution with timeout and error handling
evidence.why: Provider adapter supports full-tool agent execution while boundary audit prevents mutations

risks: If boundary audit is bypassed or disabled, evidence agents could corrupt target projects with mutations
Codex provider failures in dispatch logs suggest provider compatibility issues that could affect boundary detection
Evidence agent execution requires explicit allowAgentDispatch=true authorization, but misconfiguration could skip this check
missing: Verification that boundary audit runs in all provider execution modes (claude, codex, custom)
Evidence that boundary detection works when agents spawn subprocesses that modify files
Documentation on boundary audit performance characteristics for large repositories

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is verified in code and tests. The system correctly detects project mutations, generates blockers, and prevents PRD readiness. Acceptance criteria met: explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on target-project mutation.

agent_results.claim: INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects and blocks project file mutations from full-tool Claude agents. The explorer's evidence is accurate and comprehensive.

evidence.summary: fileDigest() uses SHA-256, buildBoundarySnapshot() creates digests for all project files excluding allowed roots
evidence.why: Confirms explorer's claim: SHA-256 digests enable exact change detection

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after maps to detect added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: Independent verification: the comparison logic correctly identifies all mutation types

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshots taken before/after agent execution loop; changes diffed and stored in result.boundary.changes
evidence.why: Verifies explorer's claim: runtime boundary enforcement wraps agent execution

evidence.summary: Boundary violations create BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers merged into readiness, forcing prd_ready=false
evidence.why: Critical verification: boundary audit blocks PRD readiness regardless of agent success

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets writes_business_code=false, agent_instruction='boundary_controlled', project_mutation='forbidden'
evidence.why: Contract-level enforcement: dispatch plan specifies forbidden mutations at protocol level

evidence.summary: Agent hard rule: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files. If verification would require mutation, report it in missing.'
evidence.why: Prompt-level enforcement: agents receive explicit boundary prohibition in instructions

evidence.summary: Claude provider execution configures DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS with full CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS allowed
evidence.why: Critical verification: agents HAVE Write/Bash tools but boundary audit prevents abuse

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues() generates blockers from missing/conflicting evidence agent results
evidence.why: Verifies explorer's claim: PRD readiness inspection enforces evidence quality

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() blocks external-only evidence for existing-project facts
evidence.why: Verifies explorer's claim: brownfield facts require project-scoped evidence

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' - agent writes file, boundary.violated, status=blocked
evidence.why: Test verification: boundary audit overrides passing agent results on violation

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test confirms untracked mutations detected: boundary.violated, changes include 'untracked-mutation.txt' added
evidence.why: Test verification: boundary works with both tracked and untracked files via git ls-files

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt() spawns claude/codex/custom processes with timeout, stdio piping, error handling
evidence.why: Verifies explorer's claim: provider adapter supports full-tool execution while boundary audit prevents mutations

risks: If boundary snapshot creation fails silently (git error or filesystem race), agents could mutate undetected
Large repos with >10k files may slow boundary snapshot creation, affecting user experience
Concurrent agent dispatches to same project root could race if boundary snapshots overlap
missing: Evidence that boundary audit handles filesystem errors during snapshot creation
Evidence that boundary audit performance is acceptable for repos with >10k files
Evidence that concurrent dispatches to same project root are serialized or isolated

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all 10 pieces of explorer evidence. The boundary audit implementation is correct: SHA-256 digests, before/after comparison, blocker generation, and PRD readiness enforcement. Full-tool agents have Write/Bash access but boundary audit prevents mutations. Tests verify behavior. Acceptance criteria met: all agents cite project paths and boundary audit blocks PRD readiness on mutations.

agent_results.claim: VERIFIED: Evidence from explorer and cross-checker is sufficient to support PRD readiness. All claims are backed by project-scoped evidence from code and tests. No assumptions are presented as facts. Missing data is properly identified.

evidence.summary: Evidence dispatch artifact root contains explorer and cross-checker results with 22 total evidence citations from project code and tests
evidence.why: Evidence gathering completed and persisted to artifact store

evidence.summary: Boundary audit implementation verified by both agents: SHA-256 digests, before/after comparison, blocker generation
evidence.why: Core claim supported by direct code evidence at cited lines

evidence.summary: PRD readiness inspection enforces evidence quality via evidenceResultBlockingIssues() and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues()
evidence.why: Router logic verifies evidence protocol is enforced

evidence.summary: Tests confirm boundary blocks mutations even when agent results pass, including untracked files
evidence.why: Behavioral verification that boundary audit works as specified

evidence.summary: Provider adapter supports full-tool agent execution (Write/Bash tools available)
evidence.why: Verifies agents have full tool access while boundary audit prevents mutations

evidence.summary: Explorer result: 10 evidence pieces, 0 assumptions, 3 risks, 3 missing items identified
evidence.why: Explorer completed with comprehensive evidence and no assumptions promoted to facts

evidence.summary: Cross-checker result: 12 evidence pieces, independent verification of all explorer claims
evidence.why: Cross-checker independently confirmed explorer evidence without conflicts

risks: Evidence confirms boundary audit works in tested scenarios, but large repos (>10k files) performance is unverified
Concurrent dispatches to same project root could race if snapshots overlap
Subprocess mutations from agents may not be detected if they occur outside snapshot window
missing: Evidence that boundary audit handles filesystem errors during snapshot creation gracefully
Performance benchmarks for boundary snapshot creation on repos with >10k files
Evidence that concurrent dispatches to same project are serialized or isolated

result.notes: Verification protocol satisfied: (1) No assumptions presented as facts - all evidence is project-scoped from code/tests. (2) Evidence paths are usable and verified - 22 citations across src/demand/, src/runtime/, and __tests__/. (3) Missing data is properly identified in missing/risks arrays and not promoted to facts. Acceptance criteria met: all three agents cite project paths, boundary audit keeps PRD blocked on mutations, evidence protocol enforced via router. Ready for PRD phase with acknowledged gaps in performance and concurrent execution scenarios.
